trajectory.description: Observation: Starting from scratch. Analysis: Need to establish a baseline and measure iteration time. I will implement a standard ResNet50 model pretrained on ImageNet, fine-tuned with class weights to handle the heavy class imbalance. I will train for 2 epochs to quickly get a baseline score and measure the time taken per epoch. Decision: Write train.py and predict.py, train for 2 epochs, and submit for evaluation.

trajectory.description: Observation: The baseline ResNet50 achieved 0.3655 balanced accuracy on the test set, but 0.8432 on the validation set (source domains). Analysis: There is a massive domain gap between the training locations and the test location. ResNet50 is overfitting to the source domains. Decision: Switch to a stronger backbone with better out-of-the-box domain generalization: CLIP ViT-B/16. I will also add strong data augmentations (ColorJitter, RandomAffine) to encourage invariance to lighting and background changes. I will train for 5 epochs to evaluate the improvement.

trajectory.description: Observation: Fine-tuning the entire CLIP ViT-B/16 model resulted in severe overfitting to the source domains (val acc 0.66, test acc 0.22), performing worse than the ResNet50 baseline. Analysis: Fine-tuning a large ViT on a small dataset destroys its pre-trained representations, leading to poor domain generalization. Decision: Switch to DINOv2 ViT-B/14, which is known for excellent frozen features. I will freeze the backbone and only train a linear classifier head. This should preserve the generalizable features and train much faster. I will train for 5 epochs to evaluate.

trajectory.description: Observation: DINOv2 frozen features achieved 0.4773 balanced accuracy, a huge improvement over ResNet50 and fine-tuned CLIP. The model is still learning (val acc improving). Analysis: We can improve this further by: 1) Training longer (15 epochs), 2) Using a better LR schedule (CosineAnnealingLR) with a slightly higher LR for the linear head, 3) Concatenating the CLS token with mean-pooled patch tokens for richer features, and 4) Adding Test-Time Augmentation (TTA) in predict.py. Decision: Update train.py and predict.py with these improvements, train for 15 epochs, and submit for evaluation.

trajectory.description: Observation: DINOv2 with frozen features achieved 0.4987 balanced accuracy. The model is still overfitting to the source domains (val acc 0.76 vs test acc 0.49). Analysis: To improve domain generalization, we need stronger regularization to prevent the linear classifier from memorizing source-specific features (like backgrounds). Decision: I will add Mixup (alpha=0.2), Label Smoothing (0.1), Dropout (0.3) before the classifier, and RandomResizedCrop to the training pipeline. I will also increase weight decay to 0.1. Since these augmentations make the task harder, I will increase training to 20 epochs. This should take ~55 minutes, well within the 100 minutes remaining.
